item.title: Ad ban for lab gems is diamonds’ best friend
item.description: UK watchdog rules jewellers cannot use ‘diamond’ in isolation in synthetic stones’ adverts

item.title: FirstFT: China’s tech giants miss out on AI stock market rally
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: US Treasury secretary meets Japanese PM, and US inflation jumps to 3.8%

item.title: Resignation of US food and drug regulator head adds to mounting agency vacancies
item.description: Lawyer Kyle Diamantas appointed FDA’s acting leader as concerns mount over hantavirus outbreak

item.title: China’s big tech groups miss out on AI stock market frenzy
item.description: Tencent and Alibaba left behind as investors focus on pure AI plays

item.title: Can GameStop buy eBay?
item.description: What happens when a small company tries to buy a big company

item.title: Musk made ‘hair-raising’ demands for control of OpenAI, Altman testifies
item.description: Start-up’s CEO takes the stand in legal battle with billionaire

item.title: Stellantis and Ford turn to partnerships to tackle Europe woes
item.description: Carmakers seek tie-ups with Chinese groups and traditional rivals to secure scale and technology

item.title: A reluctant Intertek risks testing investors’ patience
item.description: FTSE 100 company has rejected previous EQT takeover offers saying they undervalued it

item.title: Blue Owl retail fundraising evaporates amid private credit concerns
item.description: Fund it operates took in fraction of capital from year-ago level as worries mount over potential surge in loan defaults

item.title: How architecture can save lives
item.description: Carefully designed homes in Tanzania have been shown to lower disease rates among children

item.title: Group linked to Trump sons asks US for extra $400mn for tungsten mine
item.description: Cove Kaz Capital has already secured up to $1.6bn in government support for project in Kazakhstan

item.title: Trump postpones beef tariff cuts over fears of hitting US cattle farmers
item.description: Abrupt change of plan signals chaotic approach to trade

item.title: Allianz argues lawsuit against Palestine activists is ‘appropriate’
item.description: Insurance company says protests at UK offices caused employees ‘anxiety, distress and intimidation’

item.title: CME plans to launch futures market for AI computing power
item.description: New contracts will allow traders and companies to bet on and hedge future price of GPU rental

item.title: Widening control of patient records risks opening ‘Pandora’s Box’ for data, warn GPs
item.description: British Medical Association will encourage people to opt out of sharing if family doctors are replaced as controller of records

item.title: Scottish Mortgage values SpaceX at $1.25tn ahead of blockbuster IPO
item.description: UK investment trust’s valuation means its holding in Elon Musk’s company is worth £3bn

item.title: Ukraine nears deal with Pentagon to test drones in US
item.description: Draft statement of intent seen as first step towards potential bigger ‘drone deal’

item.title: Hayek family defeats activist investor’s latest push to join Swatch board
item.description: Watchmaker’s controlling shareholders use dominant voting stake to block Steven Wood’s fresh bid to win seat

item.title: Panasonic battery business boosted by expected EV gains for Tesla
item.description: Japanese group set to benefit as it forecasts its leading customer will increase market share

item.title: Delivery Hero chief to step down amid activist pressure
item.description: Founder Niklas Östberg will leave German food delivery group by next March

item.title: Why Americans dread AI
item.description: Silicon Valley encourages the view that the technology is unstoppable — and Trump seems to agree

item.title: Can Europe ‘seize the opportunity’ in nuclear fusion?
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: one of the world’s best-funded fusion companies applies to connect to the US electricity grid

item.title: Pimco vs Apollo: public aggro over private prices
item.description: But maybe they aren’t so different after all

item.title: EBay rejects $56bn GameStop bid as ‘neither credible nor attractive’
item.description: Rebuff could spur video game retailer’s chief Ryan Cohen to launch hostile bid for online marketplace

item.title: FirstFT: Starmer vows to fight on
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Warsh’s Fed in-tray and hedge funds pile into biofuel commodities

item.title: Chipmaker Cerebras joins OpenAI’s inner circle — for a price
item.description: Launching into the magic of the Altman-osphere could prove to be quite a windfall

item.title: Lotus offers lifeline to troubled UK car plant
item.description: Chief executive slashes sales target and unveils strategy to revive struggling manufacturer controlled by China’s Geely

item.title: EU to stockpile key drugs to avoid repeat of pandemic-era panic buying
item.description: Member states agree on Critical Medicines Act that aims to incentivise domestic production

item.title: Canary Wharf returns to profit as office values recover
item.description: Owner of London district makes first profit since the pandemic as workers return and investment in estate begins to bear fruit

item.title: Investment group EQT submits ‘final’ £10.6bn offer for Intertek
item.description: FTSE 100 testing group comes under mounting pressure from shareholders to engage with buyout firm
